reforms.description: The Italian government introduced a new system of tax incentives aimed at reducing labour costs, which includes abolishing social security contributions for health services made by employers and providing tax breaks for business start-ups and firms increasing hiring and R&D activities.
reforms.component_name: Tax Incentives for Hiring and R&D
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing labour costs and incentivizing R&D, the reform is expected to stimulate employment and innovation in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government committed to implementing the tax incentives as part of the labour agreement.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the approach to labour costs and R&D funding, with potential long-term impacts on employment and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A new system of tax incentives... will be given to business start-ups by young persons... and to enterprises stepping up hirings, training and research and development.
